SEARCH
Package dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement

Class JkVersion

java.lang.Object
dev.jeka.core.api.depmanagement.JkVersion
All Implemented Interfaces:
Comparable<JkVersion>
public final class JkVersion extends Object implements Comparable<JkVersion>
Used to specify a module version. Versions are comparable.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final String
    SNAPSHOT_SUFIX
     
    static final JkVersion
    UNSPECIFIED
    Mention that the version is unspecified

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    compareTo(JkVersion other)
     
    boolean
    equals(Object obj)
     
    String
    getBlock(int index)
    If version name is formatted of blocks separated with '.', this methods returns the block at specified index.
    String
    getValue()
    Returns the value of the version.
    boolean
    hasBlockAt(int index)
    Returns true when getBlock(int) won't throw an IllegalArgumentException.
    int
    hashCode()
     
    boolean
    isDigitsOnly()
     
    boolean
    isDynamic()
    Returns true if the definition stands for a dynamic version (as "1.4.+", "[1.0,2.0[", "3.0-SNAPSHOT", ...) or false if it stands for a fixed one (as "1.4.0, "2.0.3-23654114", ...).
    boolean
    isDynamicAndResolvable()
    Returns true if the definition stands for dynamic resolvable version (as 1.4.+, [1.0, 2.0[, ...).
    Returns false if the version is static or snapshot (as 1.4.0, 3.1-SNAPSHOT) A snapshot is not considered as 'resolvable'.
    boolean
    isGreaterThan(JkVersion other)
    Returns true if this version is to be considered superior to the specified one.
    boolean
    isSnapshot()
    Returns true if this version stands for a snapshot one.
    boolean
    isUnspecified()
    Returns true if this version range is unspecified.
    static JkVersion
    of(String name)
    Creates a JkVersion with the specified value.
    List<String>
    toItems()
     
    JkVersion
    toSnapshot()
     
    String
    toString()

  • Field Details

    • UNSPECIFIED

      public static final JkVersion UNSPECIFIED
      Mention that the version is unspecified

    • SNAPSHOT_SUFIX

      public static final Comparator<String> VERSION_COMPARATOR

  • Method Details

    • of

      public static JkVersion of(String name)
      Creates a JkVersion with the specified value. If specified name is null, then it creates an UNSPECIFIED version.

    • getValue

      public String getValue()
      Returns the value of the version.

    • isSnapshot

      public boolean isSnapshot()
      Returns true if this version stands for a snapshot one.

    • toSnapshot

      public JkVersion toSnapshot()

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(JkVersion other)
      Specified by:
      compareTo in interface Comparable<JkVersion>

    • isGreaterThan

      public boolean isGreaterThan(JkVersion other)
      Returns true if this version is to be considered superior to the specified one.

    • toItems

      public List<String> toItems()

    • isDigitsOnly

      public boolean isDigitsOnly()

    • isDynamic

      public boolean isDynamic()
      Returns true if the definition stands for a dynamic version (as "1.4.+", "[1.0,2.0[", "3.0-SNAPSHOT", ...) or false if it stands for a fixed one (as "1.4.0, "2.0.3-23654114", ...).

    • isUnspecified

      public boolean isUnspecified()
      Returns true if this version range is unspecified.

    • isDynamicAndResolvable

      public boolean isDynamicAndResolvable()
      Returns true if the definition stands for dynamic resolvable version (as 1.4.+, [1.0, 2.0[, ...).
      Returns false if the version is static or snapshot (as 1.4.0, 3.1-SNAPSHOT) A snapshot is not considered as 'resolvable'.

    • getBlock

      public String getBlock(int index)
      If version name is formatted of blocks separated with '.', this methods returns the block at specified index. throw IllegalArgumentException if no such block found at specified index.

    • hasBlockAt

      public boolean hasBlockAt(int index)
      Returns true when getBlock(int) won't throw an IllegalArgumentException.

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
